title: The European Spirit of Youth Orchestra opened the year at the Politeama Rossetti. An interview with the founding maestro.
body:
The European Spirit of Youth Orchestra took center stage at the New Year’s concert at the Politeama Rossetti in Trieste (Italy). Founded by Trieste-born Igor Kuret Coretti, the orchestra has, over more than 30 years of activity, involved and launched over 3,000 young musicians from across Europe. Many artists who trained with the ESYO now perform on the world’s most prestigious stages.
One of the most significant moments in the orchestra’s life is the period of intense rehearsals leading up to concerts. The next gathering for the young musicians will take place in spring in the Italian and Slovenian Collio region, where the talented performers from across the continent will be hosted by local winemakers in the Collio/Brda area. A series of concerts will follow. The project is part of the GO!2025 programme, effectively extending its spirit.
author: Source: RAI Friuli Venezia Giulia

title: ESYO Summer Tour 2025: "UNITED TOGETHER – GO! 2025, for a Europe of good friends"
body:
From July 13 to August 3, 2025, the European Spirit of Youth Orchestra (ESYO) sets off on its exciting Summer Tour 2025, proudly titled: "UNITED TOGETHER – GO! 2025, for a Europe of good friends". This year’s tour brings together young musicians from 15 countries, united by music and friendship. Representing the true spirit of Europe—and beyond—our orchestra includes talents from: Romania, Spain, Russian Federation, Serbia, Italy, North Macedonia, Hungary, Germany, Slovenia, Belarus, Poland, Slovakia, Montenegro, Portugal, and China.
Tour dates and concert details are now available online – check out the full schedule right here on our website and join us on this unforgettable musical journey across Europe!

title: United in Music: ESYO's 30th Anniversary Tour from the Balkans to Brussels
body:
Thanks to the support of the Department of Cultural and Tourism Policies of the Municipality of Trieste, co-organizer of the tour, 13 young musicians from ESYO, hailing from Italy, Albania, North Macedonia, and Serbia, will meet on October 27 in Belgrade to prepare the musical program for their concerts scheduled between November 1 and 8 in the Serbian capital, Rijeka, and Brussels.
With the tour "United Together, from the Balkans to Brussels," the young musicians of ESYO, led by Maestro Igor Coretti Kuret, who, as the great violinist Yehudi Menuhin described them, are “the connective tissue of our humanity and bearers of the message of understanding, compassion, and love, generating vibrations that no barrier can stop, and who, through their music-making, exhibit a joy that can only arise from goodwill and trust among people from diverse backgrounds,” will also give voice to those who do not have one. By playing the instruments of the Orchestra of the Sea, constructed in Italian prisons from the wood of migrant boats stranded in Lampedusa, provided for the occasion by the Foundation of Spirit and Arts chaired by Arnoldo Mosca Mondadori, and accompanied by the narrative voice of Paolo Rumiz, they will bring the endless tragedy affecting humanity, always provoked by wars, to the attention of European public opinion.
